_comment: Cross-language parity vectors for Phase C Rust ↔ Python byte-identical guarantee. Per SPEC §7.4 + §8.10 + §11.6. Loaded by both pytest (titan_plugin tests) and cargo test (titan-rust crates). DO NOT EDIT manually after a vector ships — bump spec_version + parity vector set together.

frame._comment: HMAC-SHA256 vectors for the connection-handshake response. Per SPEC §8 + B.2 + RFC 4231. Used by titan-core::frame::compute_hmac AND titan_plugin/core/_frame.compute_hmac — must produce byte-identical output.

rfc4231.name: test_case_2
rfc4231.data_ascii: what do ya want for nothing?

titan_canonical.name: kernel_authkey_handshake_t1
titan_canonical.context: Simulates a kernel-rs sending challenge to api_subprocess, who responds with HMAC(authkey, challenge). Authkey here is a known fixed value for vector reproducibility — production authkeys are HKDF-derived per Titan. Expected hex computed by titan-core::frame::compute_hmac() at first run + locked here; pytest must produce same bytes.

authkey._comment: HKDF-SHA256 vectors for bus authkey derivation per SPEC §3.1 D06. Used by titan-core::authkey::derive_bus_authkey AND titan_plugin/core/bus_authkey.derive_bus_authkey — byte-identical.

titan_canonical.name: authkey_derivation
titan_canonical.context: Derive bus authkey from a fixed identity_secret using AUTHKEY_HKDF_SALT=b'titan-bus-v1' + AUTHKEY_HKDF_INFO=b'titan-bus' + AUTHKEY_BYTES=32. Per rFP_phase_c_bus_authkey_contract_fix.md (2026-05-05): info is the CONSTANT b'titan-bus' (not titan_id). Per-Titan isolation comes from per-Titan identity secrets, NOT from info. The 'titan_id' field below is informational only; it does NOT enter the HKDF derivation. Verifies Rust + Python produce same 32-byte key.

shm_layout._comment: Slot byte layouts per SPEC §7.0 v1.0.0 (16-byte fixed header + 3 × (16-byte buffer meta + N-byte payload)). Header struct = '<QII' (LE u64 header_seq, u32 schema, u32 capacity = 16 bytes). Per-buffer meta struct = '<QII' (LE u64 wall_ns, u32 payload_bytes, u32 buffer_crc32 = 16 bytes). Total slot size = 16 + 3·(16+N) = 64 + 3·N. fastbus.bin is excluded — self-contained SPSC ring with its own 64-byte ring header. D-SPEC-35 v0.2.0→v1.0.0 MAJOR bump.

fastbus.bin._note: EXCLUDED from §7.0 universal header — self-contained SPSC ring (64-byte ring header + 1024 × 256-byte slots = 262208).

bus_specs._comment: Subset of SPEC §8 catalog for parity check. Full 47-message catalog populates in C2-2. Verifies Rust phf::Map<&str, BusMsgSpec> agrees with Python bus_specs.py for priority + coalesce_by + catalog category.

adoption_payload._comment: B.2.1 supervision-transfer ADOPTION_REQUEST + ADOPTION_ACK byte-locked vectors per SPEC §8.4 + §8.4.1 (LOCKED in v0.1.5 — earlier 79-byte target was an unvalidated estimate; canonical Python ground truth at worker_swap_handler.py:206-216 produces 60 bytes for the request payload). Loaded by both Python pytest (tests/parity/test_adoption_payload.py) and cargo test (titan-trinity-daemon::adoption::tests).
adoption_payload.schema_correction_v0_1_5: Earlier SPEC §8.4 row claimed payload includes rid (5 fields). Python ground truth puts rid in the bus envelope (top-level msg dict, set by bus.make_msg(rid=...)), NOT inside the payload. Payload itself has 4 fields. SPEC §8.4 row + §8.4.1 corrected in v0.1.5; Decision Log D-SPEC-31 entry.

request.encoder_options: msgpack.packb(payload, use_bin_type=False) — Python; rmpv::encode::write_value with insertion-order Vec<(Value, Value)> — Rust

ack_accepted.encoder_options_clarification: msgpack default use_bin_type=True (Python ≥ 1.0 default; matches Rust rmpv default). 36-char UUID encodes as str8 marker `d9` + 1-byte length, NOT the older str16 `da` + 2-byte length used by use_bin_type=False legacy mode. Verified against Python wire output 2026-04-30.

outer_body_5d._comment: C-S6 outer-body parity vectors. Each vector: input msgpack source dict (sensor_cache_outer_body payload) + topology[0:10] outer_lower bytes + UNIFIED_SPIRIT_FILTER_DOWN.outer_body[5] multipliers + OUTER_SPIRIT_FILTER_DOWN.outer_body[5] multipliers -> expected outer_body_5d.bin payload bytes (5 x float32 LE = 20). Logic-source: titan_plugin/logic/outer_trinity.py:_collect_outer_body. Populated in C6-3 by tests/parity/gen_outer_body_vectors.py.

outer_mind_15d._comment: C-S6 outer-mind parity vectors. Same shape as outer_body_5d but 15 dims; ground_up applies to willing[10:15] ONLY per G10. Logic-source: titan_plugin/logic/outer_mind_tensor.py:collect_outer_mind_15d + outer_trinity._collect_outer_mind. Populated in C6-4.

outer_spirit_45d._comment: C-S6 outer-spirit parity vectors. Inputs: outer_body_5d slot bytes + outer_mind_15d slot bytes (Observer Principle reads) + sensor_cache_outer_spirit msgpack pre-aggregated outer-state. Output: outer_spirit_45d.bin payload (45 x float32 LE = 180; SAT[0:15]+CHIT[15:30]+ANANDA[30:45]; Material aspects). Observer dims [0:5] present in slot but masked at filter_down emit per G8. Logic-source: titan_plugin/logic/outer_spirit_tensor.py:collect_outer_spirit_45d. Populated in C6-5.

outer_spirit_filter_down_msg._comment: C-S6 OUTER_SPIRIT_FILTER_DOWN msgpack payload parity vectors. Input: outer_spirit_45d slot bytes (the full 45D). Output: msgpack-encoded {multipliers: {outer_body[5], outer_mind[15], outer_spirit_content[40]}, ts}. Asserts outer_spirit_content is exactly outer_spirit_45d[5:45] byte-identical (length 40, observer dims [0:5] EXCLUDED per G8 + §10.F step 3). Populated in C6-5.

fastbus_layout._comment: Lock-free SPSC ring header byte layout per SPEC §7.1 fastbus.bin (v0.1.4) + PLAN_microkernel_phase_c_s3_substrate.md §9. Loaded by titan-fastbus tests (Rust) AND optional Python attach-as-validator (C-S3 chunk C3-7 e2e).
fastbus_layout._offsets_comment: v0.1.4 reordered AtomicU64 fields (read_idx, write_idx) to 8-byte aligned offsets. Same 64-byte total + same field set as v0.1.3, just reordered for portable lock-free atomics.

schumann_periods_ns._comment: Schumann frequency periods in nanoseconds per Preamble G13 LOCKED (NOT tunable). Loaded by titan-schumann tests (Rust) — verifies generator computes period_ns = 1e9 / hz to integer truncation. Tolerance ±1ns for IEEE 754 round-trip.
schumann_periods_ns._relations_comment: spirit_period × 9 ≈ body_period (relative phase ratio ≈ 1.000003); mind_period × 3 ≈ body_period (≈ 0.99996). All 3 generators share a common epoch_t0; phase synchrony preserved within ~1µs over short windows.

ground_up_nudge._comment: Byte-identical port verification for ground_up.py compute_nudge (lines 51-101) per Preamble G5 + G10. Reference values computed by Python ground_up.py with these inputs. Loaded by both pytest (Python self-verify) AND titan-trinity-rs::ground_up tests. Tolerance abs=1e-6 for f32 IEEE 754 + Python round(_, 6) on total_magnitude.

cases.name: zero_signal
cases._comment: Zero grounding signal → zero nudge everywhere

cases.name: saturated_signal
cases._comment: Signal=1.0 + prev=0.0 + damping=0.95 → damped = 0.05*1.0 = 0.05 (within ±0.05 clamp). total_mag = sqrt(10 × 0.0025) = 0.158113883...
